description: A bare return inside a non-lambda proc returns from the enclosing method, breaking control flow unexpectedly.
body: Failure mode: code stores or passes a Proc (not a lambda) containing a return, and when it runs the return jumps out of the method that created the proc rather than just the proc, skipping later code or returning a wrong value. Detection signals: a method exits earlier than expected when a stored proc runs; a LocalJumpError if the creating method already returned; return used inside Proc.new/proc rather than a lambda. Do not rely on a proc's return behaving like a method's. Safer fallback: use a lambda (->) when the callable needs a local return and strict arity, reserve procs/blocks for cases without an explicit return, and test the callable's return value and control flow.
